questions.question: What is the imaginary unit i?
options: A Greek letter used in mathematics
questions.explanation: The imaginary unit i is defined such that i² = -1. This extends real numbers to solve equations that have no real solutions.

questions.question: In the complex number 3 - 2i, what is the imaginary part?
questions.explanation: In a complex number a + bi, the imaginary part is the coefficient b (not the term bi). Here, b = -2.

questions.question: Calculate i³.
questions.explanation: i³ = i² × i = (-1) × i = -i. Powers of i cycle: i, -1, -i, 1, i, -1, ...

questions.question: Add: (2 + 3i) + (1 - i) = ?
questions.explanation: Add real parts: 2 + 1 = 3. Add imaginary parts: 3i - i = 2i. Result: 3 + 2i.

questions.question: Multiply: (1 + i)(1 - i) = ?
questions.explanation: Use the formula (a+b)(a-b) = a² - b². Here: 1² - i² = 1 - (-1) = 1 + 1 = 2. These are complex conjugates!

questions.question: Which statement is true about complex numbers?
options: All real numbers are complex numbers
Complex numbers can only be added, not multiplied
The imaginary unit i is a real number
questions.explanation: Every real number is a complex number of the form a + 0i. Option 1 is false (real numbers are complex too). Option 3 is false (complex numbers support all operations). Option 4 is false (i is not real).
